target.title: [Feature]: /label slash command & /new <name> session naming for WebChat/Control UI
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

This PR lets WebChat/Control UI users name dashboard sessions from the browser without adding a new public session-naming contract. `/new <name>` creates a new dashboard session with the existing `label` field, and `/label <name>` is kept as a Control UI-local alias that patches the active session `label`.

Why does this matter now?

Issue #93422 asks for named WebChat/Control UI sessions. A review comment also pointed at the merged `/name <title>` work, so this update aligns with that merged direction by reusing `label` instead of introducing a competing writable `displayName` field.

What is the intended outcome?

Users can type `/new Research Plan` to create and switch to a dashboard session labelled `Research Plan`, then type `/label Follow Up` to relabel that active session. The session selector and Recent list show the same label while the underlying session key stays unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This does not change session key generation, config shape, migrations, auth, provider routing, Swift/native gateway protocol models, CLI session output, or the shared/native slash-command registry. `/label` is intentionally scoped to Control UI, while the merged shared `/name` command remains the main cross-surface naming command.

What does success look like?

A named session created from the real browser UI remains a normal dashboard session, uses the existing `label` metadata, avoids sending a non-persisted fallback main session as `parentSessionKey`, and displays the chosen label consistently in the Control UI selector and Recent list.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please focus on the Control UI-only command boundary, the reuse of the existing `label` field, and the parent-session guard for fresh/fallback main sessions.

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix at the Control UI session lifecycle boundary, aligned with the existing session `label` source of truth.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High. The patch now has real browser proof plus focused regression coverage for `/new <name>`, `/label <name>`, selected-agent scoping, busy-session handling, and the fallback-main parent guard.
- Root cause: Control UI could render a fallback `Main Session` even when `sessions.list` had no persisted row, then `/new <name>` could send `agent:main:main` as a parent the gateway rejected. The earlier PR shape also added a separate naming contract instead of reusing the merged `label` direction.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The create path now treats an empty sessions list plus an agent main key as a fallback UI row, so it omits `parentSessionKey` until a persisted parent exists. The naming path writes `label`, which is already the merged naming field, instead of adding protocol/gateway/CLI writable `displayName` expansion.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users can create and rename named dashboard sessions from Control UI without hitting `unknown parent session: agent:main:main` on a fresh gateway, and maintainers do not need to evaluate a competing public naming field.
- What did NOT change: Existing session keys, `/new` without an argument, shared `/name`, gateway protocol schema, native protocol models, CLI session projection, authentication, provider routing, config, and migrations remain unchanged.
- Related open PR scan: #93587 is the existing author PR for #93422. The naming-contract work called out in review, #88581, is already merged, so this update aligns #93587 with that merged `label` direction instead of creating or preserving a competing open contract.
- Merge-risk explanation: automation risk is limited to Control UI-local slash command dispatch; availability risk is limited to the existing disconnected-gateway error surface; security-boundary risk is limited to patching the current scoped session key; session-state risk is limited to deciding whether the visible current session is a real persisted parent.
- Why this risk is acceptable: the patch does not add backend command execution, auth scope, protocol schema, config, migration, or native-client behavior. The only new write path calls existing `sessions.patch` for `label`, and the parent guard is covered for unknown, non-persisted, empty-list fallback, persisted-row, and stale non-empty list states.
- Patch quality notes: The parent guard is limited to unknown, explicitly non-persisted, or empty-list fallback main state. Existing persisted rows and stale non-empty session lists keep their previous parent behavior.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #93422

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #93587 review feedback requested real Control UI proof for `/new <name>`, `/label <name>`, and session picker behavior. Related #88581 is the merged shared `/name <title>` direction this update now aligns with.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

This is issue-driven contributor work. The current update also addresses bot review feedback by supplying real browser proof and by removing the competing writable `displayName` contract from the PR shape.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI `/new <name>` creates a labelled dashboard session, `/label <name>` relabels that session, and the session picker/Recent list show the updated label.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw dev gateway with browser-driven Control UI, connected using a private gateway token with secrets redacted from this PR body.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Opened Control UI, connected to the local gateway, typed `/new Browser Label Alpha` in the chat composer and sent it, confirmed the current session selector and Recent list switched to `Browser Label Alpha`, then typed `/label Browser Label Renamed` in that new session and sent it.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live output / terminal capture from the redacted browser proof and focused validation observed these user-visible states:

  ```text
  after /new Browser Label Alpha:
    current session selector: Browser Label Alpha
    Recent sessions first item: Browser Label Alpha
    session key shape: agent:main:dashboard:*
    gateway sessions.create: success

  after /label Browser Label Renamed:
    current session selector: Browser Label Renamed
    Recent sessions first item: Browser Label Renamed

  persisted session entry for the created dashboard session:
    label: Browser Label Renamed
    parentSessionKey: agent:main:main
    hasSessionId: true

  focused validation result on committed HEAD:
    Test Files  5 passed (5)
    Tests       271 passed (271)
    exit_code   0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: `/new Browser Label Alpha` created and selected a dashboard session labelled `Browser Label Alpha`; `/label Browser Label Renamed` updated the same selected session so both the selector and Recent sessions list showed `Browser Label Renamed`. The persisted session entry has `label: Browser Label Renamed` and a real session id.
- What was not tested: I did not test a remote hosted gateway, mobile/native clients, or the merged shared `/name` command in this proof pass.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The browser proof used a local development gateway, so private gateway token and provider details are intentionally omitted. The visible proof above is copied from the redacted browser/session-state observation rather than exposing local artifact paths or secrets.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the fallback-main parent fix, the same real Control UI `/new Browser Label Alpha` path failed with `GatewayRequestError: unknown parent session: agent:main:main` because the UI could send the fallback `Main Session` key as `parentSessionKey` before a real main session entry existed.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
corepack pnpm exec vitest run ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts src/auto-reply/command-control.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added and updated Control UI coverage for `/new <name>` passing a `label`, `/label <name>` patching the current session `label`, selected-agent global scoping, busy-session immediate `/label` behavior, local-only `/label` command registration, and the empty-list fallback main session parent guard. Existing coverage still verifies persisted rows and stale non-empty session lists keep parent behavior.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The real browser `/new Browser Label Alpha` flow failed with `GatewayRequestError: unknown parent session: agent:main:main` when Control UI had a fallback main key but no persisted main session row.

If no test was added, why not?

Regression tests were added for the new label flow and the fallback-main parent case.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Control UI users can name new dashboard sessions with `/new <name>` and relabel the active session with `/label <name>`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The change reuses existing session `label` metadata and does not require configuration or migration steps.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The browser proof used private local credentials, but the patch does not change auth, secrets handling, network policy, or tool execution behavior.

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is the Control UI session lifecycle: distinguishing a visible fallback main session from a persisted parent session while preserving parent behavior for real session rows.

How is that risk mitigated?

The guard is narrowly scoped to unknown sessions, explicit non-persisted current rows, or an empty session list with an agent main key. Focused tests cover the fallback case and preserve the stale non-empty list behavior, and real browser proof confirms the fresh Control UI flow now creates and relabels a session successfully.

Why is this risk acceptable?

The change stays inside Control UI session selection and uses the existing `label` write path. It does not add backend command execution, auth scope, protocol schema, config, migration, or native-client behavior, and it keeps persisted parent behavior intact for real session rows.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after the branch/body update.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Author-side real browser proof and focused regression validation are supplied above. After push, the remaining items are normal maintainer review and current-head CI results.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 Control UI-only `/label` alongside merged shared `/name`: Disclosed as an intentional UI-local alias boundary. `/label` is not added to the shared/native registry, while the merged `/name` route remains the cross-surface naming command.
- RF-002 `/new <text>` compatibility risk: Addressed by keeping bare `/new` behavior and scoping `/new <name>` to Control UI dashboard-session creation with the existing `label` field. The PR body calls out this user-visible behavior and the unchanged out-of-scope surfaces.
- RF-003 fallback-main parent-session behavior: Fixed with the parent guard and covered by focused tests for empty-list fallback main state, unknown/non-persisted rows, persisted rows, and stale non-empty lists. Real browser proof confirms `sessions.create` succeeds instead of failing with `unknown parent session: agent:main:main`.
- RF-004 maintainer product/session-state acceptance: Disclosed as the remaining maintainer decision. The code change is limited to Control UI-local command semantics and existing `label` metadata so maintainers can evaluate the narrower product tradeoff.
- RF-005 Control UI naming command documentation: Addressed in the Control UI command palette metadata for `/label` with description and args, while public cross-surface naming docs remain aligned to the merged `/name` command rather than introducing a second shared command contract.

target.title: fix: preserve cron delivery awareness for target sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes isolated cron deliveries so the channel that receives a scheduled delivery also gets ephemeral context awareness for the next agent turn.
- Keeps the existing user-visible delivery content unchanged: transcript mirrors and channel sends still contain only the delivered message body, while cron metadata stays in system-event context.
- Covers direct/automatic cron delivery, verified message-tool delivery, and explicit off-plan message-tool sends that visibly delivered to a channel.
- Also records target-channel awareness for delivery failures when a scheduled delivery reaches a channel-level failure path, so the target session can understand what happened.
- Intentionally out of scope: persisting awareness in chat history, changing webchat display, or adding user-visible cron job metadata to delivered messages.
- Success means a user can reply in the delivery channel after a cron send and the agent knows what was just delivered, without leaking cron internals into the channel message.
- Reviewers should focus on target session resolution, threaded message-tool verification, idempotency/dedupe behavior, and preserving the existing double-announce guard.

## Linked context

Closes: N/A

Related:

- Addresses #74743 - cron responses can reach the destination channel while the destination session lacks injected response context; this PR fixes the target-channel next-turn context part without auto-closing the issue before maintainer confirmation.
- #88757 - broader proactive-message/session-context desynchronization issue; this PR covers the isolated cron delivery slice.
- #80786 - merged cron direct-announce transcript mirror work; this PR adds next-turn context without relying on transcript replay.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes. This was requested from a local maintainer workflow after observing that cron deliveries reached a channel but did not enter that channel's next-turn context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: isolated cron delivery reached Weixin, but the receiving channel's next agent turn did not reliably know a scheduled delivery had happened.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw gateway using the user's existing OpenClaw state/config, Weixin channel, real scheduled cron delivery, Codex harness target session, and Pi-style/openai-completions target session.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the local code, started the gateway on port 18789 with the existing state/config, triggered scheduled cron deliveries, then replied in the same Weixin conversation asking what number/content had just been delivered.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): redacted runtime log excerpt from the local OpenClaw gateway shows awareness entering the target prompt before model execution:

```text
Codex harness, message-tool delivery:
cron artifact trace=60acc6ab-bf9c-4e81-9077-f42d8b344829 ts=2026-06-17T10:33:17.869Z
provider=deepseek model=deepseek-v4-flash modelApi=openai-completions
runId=60acc6ab-bf9c-4e81-9077-f42d8b344829 didSendViaMessagingTool=true
messagingToolSentTargets=[{"tool":"message","provider":"openclaw-weixin","to":"<weixin direct>","text":"593827"}]
messagingToolSentTexts=["593827"]

target prompt trace=24b33b5d-4e7a-4fde-9e81-15bc1d6f3306 ts=2026-06-17T10:33:35.133Z
provider=openai model=gpt-5.5 modelApi=openai-chatgpt-responses runId=cdcd6ac5-dc61-43b3-9970-00195d4d6ffc
System: [2026-06-17 18:33:17 GMT+8] A scheduled cron job delivered this message to this channel:
System: 593827
What number did you receive from the cron message tool delivery? If you didn't receive any number, just say you didn't receive one.
tool.call message {"action":"send","message":"593827"}

Pi-style/openai-completions harness, direct delivery:
target prompt trace=f0ced73b-a26d-40b8-9f57-8e5897e65cfe ts=2026-06-16T09:37:13.317Z
provider=deepseek model=deepseek-v4-flash modelApi=openai-completions runId=b682d521-8c15-4289-bd48-7701396fc2a0
System: [2026-06-16 17:26:17 GMT+8] A scheduled cron job delivered this message to this channel:
System: Guangzhou weather delivery body was present here in the target prompt.
```
- Observed result after fix: the receiving channel's next agent turn had awareness of the cron-delivered content; the Weixin-visible delivery remained only the delivered payload and did not include cron job ids or scheduling metadata.
- What was not tested: live channels beyond Weixin in the local environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: awareness is intentionally an in-memory system-event queue and is consumed by the next agent turn, so it is not visible as a normal webchat/transcript row.
- Before evidence: prior behavior delivered the cron message to the channel but left no target-channel context for the next agent turn; for Codex message-tool delivery, the target reply previously said it did not receive a number.

### Harness proof logs

Codex harness, message-tool delivery path:

```text
cron artifact trace=60acc6ab-bf9c-4e81-9077-f42d8b344829 ts=2026-06-17T10:33:17.869Z
provider=deepseek model=deepseek-v4-flash modelApi=openai-completions
runId=60acc6ab-bf9c-4e81-9077-f42d8b344829 didSendViaMessagingTool=true
messagingToolSentTargets=[{"tool":"message","provider":"openclaw-weixin","to":"<weixin direct>","text":"593827"}]
messagingToolSentTexts=["593827"]

target prompt trace=24b33b5d-4e7a-4fde-9e81-15bc1d6f3306 ts=2026-06-17T10:33:35.133Z
provider=openai model=gpt-5.5 modelApi=openai-chatgpt-responses runId=cdcd6ac5-dc61-43b3-9970-00195d4d6ffc
System: [2026-06-17 18:33:17 GMT+8] A scheduled cron job delivered this message to this channel:
System: 593827
What number did you receive from the cron message tool delivery? If you didn't receive any number, just say you didn't receive one.

tool.call message {"action":"send","message":"593827"}
```

Pi-style/openai-completions harness, direct delivery path:

```text
target prompt trace=f0ced73b-a26d-40b8-9f57-8e5897e65cfe ts=2026-06-16T09:37:13.317Z
provider=deepseek model=deepseek-v4-flash modelApi=openai-completions runId=b682d521-8c15-4289-bd48-7701396fc2a0
System: [2026-06-16 17:26:17 GMT+8] A scheduled cron job delivered this message to this channel:
System: Guangzhou weather delivery body was present here in the target prompt.
```

Additional Codex direct-delivery check:

```text
target prompt trace=a4d04758-8bf9-4c6a-8736-353b76fc5ce7 ts=2026-06-17T08:00:04.947Z
provider=openai model=gpt-5.5 modelApi=openai-chatgpt-responses runId=0c0638a6-2edb-4055-97c0-c888d235e4cb
System: [2026-06-17 15:58:34 GMT+8] A scheduled cron job delivered this message to this channel:
System: random number 742531
What number did you receive from the cron message? If you didn't receive any number, just say you didn't receive one.

tool.call message {"action":"send","message":"I received cron random number 742531"}
```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` - passed, 77 tests.
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts` - passed, 52 tests.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `pnpm build` - passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - final run clean: no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.82)`.
- `CLAWSWEEPER_CODEX_LOGIN_METHOD=chatgpt pnpm local-review -- --target-dir <openclaw checkout> --target-repo openclaw/openclaw --base origin/main` - final run clean on `da94c9cf902fff9df45b8ee3b8b3d7154079311e`: `result: nothing_found`, `check_conclusion: success`.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Direct cron delivery now asserts target-session awareness is queued separately from existing main-session awareness.
- Session-bound cron delivery now asserts target-session awareness without main-session leakage.
- Verified message-tool cron delivery now queues target awareness.
- Explicit off-plan message-tool delivery now queues target awareness when a visible channel send is observed, without inheriting account/thread from the scheduled delivery target.
- Threaded message-tool delivery with implicit thread evidence now asserts awareness resolves to the actual thread session.
- Message-tool delivery whose resolved target session is the main session now still queues target awareness instead of being dropped.
- Media-only verified message-tool delivery now queues awareness using the existing transcript mirror filename projection.
- Failure-path delivery dispatch now announces failed delivery context to the resolved target session when delivery reaches the target-aware failure branch.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Live follow-up in the delivery channel lacked context that a cron delivery had just happened.
- Codex message-tool delivery could send a visible channel message, but the next target turn could still reply that it did not receive the number because the off-plan visible send was not queued as target awareness.
- The first local autoreview pass found that explicit off-plan message-tool sends could incorrectly inherit account/thread from the scheduled delivery target; this PR gates fallback account/thread use to verified target deliveries and adds regression coverage.
- Earlier ClawSweeper/local review passes found same-session and media-only message-tool awareness gaps; both are fixed, and the final local review was clean.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added/updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The next agent turn in a cron delivery target can now see the delivered message or delivery failure as system-event context. The outbound channel message content itself is unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Session targeting for awareness events, especially threaded deliveries, duplicate main-session/target-session awareness, explicit off-plan message-tool sends, and non-text message-tool deliveries.

How is that risk mitigated?

The patch reuses existing outbound session route resolution, transcript mirror projection, and idempotency keys; queues target awareness only after successful non-best-effort delivery or explicit target-aware failure handling; avoids duplicate target queueing when direct fallback already queues main-session awareness; does not add cron metadata to user-visible channel payloads; and adds focused regression tests for direct, session-bound, message-tool, off-plan explicit message-tool, same-session, media-only, failure, and threaded implicit-thread cases.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI is pending. Live Weixin proof was completed locally by the maintainer across Codex and Pi-style/openai-completions harness paths.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview found one P1 issue around explicit off-plan message-tool target resolution; this PR fixes it by avoiding scheduled-target account/thread fallback for unverified off-plan sends and adds a regression test. The final local ClawSweeper review found nothing.

target.title: fix(device-pairing): guard role normalization against non-string entries
target.body:
## Summary

`normalizeRoleList` in `src/shared/device-pairing-access.ts` called `.trim()` on every `roles[]` entry and the singular `role` **without a `typeof === "string"` guard**. The `roles`/`role` fields are loaded from disk via a blind cast (`coercePairingStateRecord`, `src/infra/pairing-files.ts`), so a malformed/legacy pairing record with a non-string entry — e.g. `roles: [123]` or `role: 5` — threw `TypeError: role.trim is not a function`, crashing `resolvePendingDeviceApprovalState`. `openclaw devices list` calls that per pending request with no surrounding try/catch (`src/cli/devices-cli.runtime.ts`), so the whole table render blows up on one bad record.

The scopes half of the same summary (`normalizeDeviceAuthScopes`) is already guarded, and the merged #90654/#92178 work added the same guard to the sibling `mergeRoles`/`mergeScopes`/`formatAuditList` in `src/infra/device-pairing.ts` — but **missed this path**.

This routes each item through the existing shared non-string-safe normalizer `normalizeUniqueSingleOrTrimmedStringList` (the exact helper `mergeRoles` uses), so non-string entries are dropped instead of crashing while valid roles are still trimmed, deduped, and sorted. Net **−10 LOC**; all three `normalizeRoleList` callers (pending request, approved device, token roles) are covered by the single fix.

### Changes
- `src/shared/device-pairing-access.ts` — route `normalizeRoleList` through the guarded shared helper
- `src/shared/device-pairing-access.test.ts` — regression tests for non-string `roles` / `role` / `token.role`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `normalizeRoleList` crashed (`TypeError: role.trim is not a function`) on a non-string role from a malformed pairing record, taking down `resolvePendingDeviceApprovalState` / `openclaw devices list`; it now drops non-string entries and classifies access normally.

Real environment tested: a direct-call harness importing the real `src/shared/device-pairing-access.ts`, run with the repo's `tsx`, Node 22.22.1, no model / provider / channel credentials and no network (the function is pure). The "before" column reverts only that one source file to `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# dp-proof.mts (~20 lines) imports resolvePendingDeviceApprovalState from the real source
# and calls it with malformed (non-string) roles/role on the pending and paired paths.
tsx dp-proof.mts                                                                      # AFTER (this branch)
git stash push -- src/shared/device-pairing-access.ts && tsx dp-proof.mts && git stash pop   # BEFORE (origin/main)
```

Evidence after fix:
```
########## AFTER FIX (this branch) ##########
OK     pending roles:[123]         -> {"kind":"new-pairing","requested":{"roles":[],"scopes":["operator.read"]},"approved":null}
OK     pending singular role:5     -> {"kind":"new-pairing","requested":{"roles":[],"scopes":["operator.read"]},"approved":null}
OK     paired (approved) roles:[7] -> {"kind":"role-upgrade","requested":{"roles":["operator"],"scopes":["operator.read"]},"approved":{"roles":[],"scopes":["operator.read"]}}
OK     valid+malformed mixed [123,'operator',null,'  admin  '] + role:5 -> {"kind":"new-pairing","requested":{"roles":["admin","operator"],"scopes":["operator.read"]},"approved":null}

########## BEFORE FIX (origin/main) ##########
THROW  pending roles:[123]         -> TypeError: role.trim is not a function
THROW  pending singular role:5     -> TypeError: item.trim is not a function
THROW  paired (approved) roles:[7] -> TypeError: role.trim is not a function
THROW  valid+malformed mixed ...   -> TypeError: role.trim is not a function
```

Observed result after fix: every non-string role entry that previously threw is now dropped, valid roles are trimmed/deduped/sorted (`["admin","operator"]`), and the approval classification returns normally — no crash on any of the three role paths.

What was not tested: end-to-end `openclaw devices list` with a hand-crafted on-disk pairing file holding a non-string role. The pure-function repro exercises the exact crash; reachability is confirmed by source (the `roles`/`role` fields reach `normalizeRoleList` unvalidated through `coercePairingStateRecord`'s blind cast, and the CLI's `parseDevicePairingList` only does `Array.isArray` checks, never element-type checks).

## Additional checks

Four cases in `device-pairing-access.test.ts` cover non-string `roles[]`, singular `role`, and `token.role` (exercising all three callers), alongside the pre-existing valid-role and revoked-token cases. Formatter, static analysis, and type checks all pass for the changed files. Mirrors the merged #90654/#92178 robustness fix; net −10 source LOC.

target.title: fix(compaction): skip replay-unsafe session sends
target.body:
Fixes #84139.

## Summary

- Filters replay-unsafe `sessions_send` artifacts at the compaction safeguard branch-fallback boundary without dropping safe assistant text from mixed assistant messages.
- Keeps the existing duplicate protections for target-side delivered inter-session turns and source-side already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result artifacts.
- Adds a focused mixed-content regression proving safe assistant text remains provider-visible while `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and results are removed.

## Origin / follow-up

- N/A — independent root-cause fix for linked issue #84139. The recorded related open PR scan reported `related_open_prs: []` and `scan_error: null`, so this is not following or competing with a broader same-contract PR found by the daily-fix scan.

## Root Cause

- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The patch is limited to the compaction safeguard branch-fallback source boundary, has focused regression coverage for the over-filtering case, and has fresh current-head production-hook terminal proof.
- **Root cause:** The root/source invariant at the branch-fallback filter was too coarse: it treated any assistant message containing a replay-unsafe `sessions_send` tool call as entirely unsafe. That removed the already-executed send artifact, but it also dropped safe assistant text from mixed text-plus-tool-call turns, losing useful fallback history during compaction recovery.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix keeps replay safety at the source boundary by removing only the `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and their matching tool results before `containsRealConversation()` and summarization see recovered branch history. Assistant messages that still contain safe content remain available for summarization; assistant messages that contain only replay-unsafe send blocks are still removed.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** A compaction that fires during an inter-agent `sessions_send` exchange should not duplicate delivered messages, but it also should not erase safe context from the source session's assistant turn. This keeps both sides of the session-state/message-delivery boundary intact.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth owner is the compaction safeguard's recovered branch-history filter, immediately before recovered messages are classified as real conversation and sent to summarization. The patch preserves that boundary by reusing `isMessagingToolSendAction()` for send-action detection and `toolCallId` linkage for matching results; no downstream summary text filtering or delivery-layer special case is added.
- **Compatibility/default behavior:** No public API, user configuration, schema, protocol, wire/data format, migration, provider routing, session persistence format, message delivery behavior, or default compaction setting changes.
- **Out of scope:** Gateway UI behavior, Mantis/Crabbox automation, third-party channel transport, provider selection, idempotency keys, session storage format, and `sessions_send` execution semantics are intentionally unchanged.
- **Patch quality notes:** The production change remains one source file and one regression file. The new `return true` branches keep non-send blocks, malformed non-tool blocks, and unrelated tool calls; the `return false` branch removes only replay-unsafe `sessions_send` blocks after recording their id for result filtering. The test names use "fallback" because they exercise the existing compaction branch-fallback seam that caused the duplicate/replay behavior.
- **What did NOT change:** Normal compaction preparation, message delivery, `sessions_send` execution, idempotency keys, provider routing, session persistence format, config, schema, API, migration behavior, and default compaction settings are unchanged.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: session-state` and `merge-risk: message-delivery`.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes which recovered session-branch entries can become compaction summary input when preparation omitted real conversation content. Over-filtering can lose useful fallback context; under-filtering can replay already-executed send artifacts.
- **Why acceptable:** Current-head proof covers both sides of the boundary: target/source `sessions_send` artifacts do not reach the provider, ordinary safe branch fallback remains provider-visible, and the new regression proves mixed assistant text is preserved while `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and results are removed.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Prevents compaction safeguard branch fallback from reusing already-executed `sessions_send` interactions as fallback history during compaction recovery while preserving safe assistant text in mixed source-session turns.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree at `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93293` on head `9ca43f2f454a524c0eb9d9e3726dfc575442961e` after `daily_fix.sh sync-main`. The terminal proof script imports the current PR's production `compactionSafeguardExtension`, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
TASK_WORKTREE="/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93293"
EVIDENCE_DIR="/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93293-evidence"
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 TASK_WORKTREE="$TASK_WORKTREE" pnpm exec tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/proof-84139-sessions-send-compaction-replay.mts")
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts)
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts)
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && git diff --check)
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts)
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[proof-84139] Production compaction safeguard hook proof for PR #93293 / issue #84139.
[proof-84139] Production paths: compactionSafeguardExtension, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection.
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: no real conversation messages to summarize; writing compaction boundary to suppress re-trigger loop.

=== Target session after sessions_send delivery ===
branch reply count before compaction: 1
compaction provider calls for target branch: 0
provider-visible target reply count: 0
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — `status: waiting on author`:** This update supplies the requested author-side repair and fresh current-head proof; label removal is left to the normal re-review flow rather than manual label mutation.
- **RF-002 — preserve safe assistant text and add regression:** Added `preserves assistant text while filtering sessions_send tool calls as fallback history`, which fails on the old whole-message filter and passes after the block-level filter.
- **RF-003 — avoid dropping mixed assistant turns:** `filterReplayUnsafeMessagingToolCalls()` now removes only replay-unsafe send blocks; safe text content remains in the assistant message.
- **RF-004 — bound over-filtering and under-filtering:** The regression proves safe text is preserved, existing source/target replay tests prove send artifacts are still removed, and the production proof shows ordinary safe branch fallback remains provider-visible.
- **RF-005 — narrow repair:** The change is mechanical and limited to the branch-fallback filter plus one focused regression; no delivery, provider, persistence, config, schema, or public-contract surface changed.
- **RF-006 — `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts:270-274`:** Replaced the whole-assistant-message `continue` with block-level filtering and kept `toolCallId` tracking for matching `sessions_send` tool results.
- **RF-007 — compaction safeguard test:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` passed with 98 tests.
- **RF-008 — related session tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts` passed with 97 tests across 3 files.
- **RF-009 — diff whitespace:** `git diff --check` passed with no output.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** Empty compaction preparation with session branch fallback filters target-side `inter_session` inputs from `sessions_send`, source-side already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result entries, and mixed assistant turns where safe text appears beside a replay-unsafe `sessions_send` tool call.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression sits at the branch-fallback seam that produced duplicate recovery and over-filtering behavior, without requiring a full multi-agent live run or changing `sessions_send` delivery code.
- **TDD RED proof:** Before the production change, the new mixed-content test failed as expected with `AssertionError: expected [ 'user' ] to deeply equal [ 'user', 'assistant' ]`, proving the old filter dropped safe assistant text.

target.title: [codex] Fix Telegram rich parse fallback text loss
target.body:
Fixes #91383.

## What changed

- Retry normal Telegram rich text sends as plain text when Telegram rejects rich parsing.
- Use the exact original source chunk for the plain retry instead of rendered HTML or separately derived fallback chunks.
- Preserve reply/thread/silent options on the fallback retry.
- Add a regression test for a normal Telegram reply whose opening text contains early Markdown links.

## Why

Telegram can reject rich/HTML parsing for messages with complex markup. The normal text send path did not have the same parse fallback protection used by caption edits, so an early-link reply could lose or corrupt the visible opening when fallback handling went sideways.

The fallback now keeps the source chunk intact, so the visible retry starts from the assistant reply's first characters instead of from the middle of a URL or Markdown link tail.

## Validation

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-plan.test.ts
git diff --check
```

I did not run a live Telegram bot repro from this machine; validation is focused source-level regression coverage for the ordinary Telegram send path.

target.title: fix(llm): honor cacheRetention for LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #37966 — `cacheRetention` configured for LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models (e.g. `litellm/claude-opus-4-6`) is silently ignored.

## Root Cause

Two code paths block LiteLLM from using Anthropic prompt caching:

1. **`resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily()`** only recognizes direct Anthropic, Bedrock, and `anthropic-messages`-API providers. LiteLLM uses the `openai-completions` API path, so it falls through to `undefined`.

2. **`detectCompat()`** in openai-completions only sets `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for OpenRouter, not LiteLLM. Without this, `cache_control` markers are never injected into the payload.

A previous PR (#38221) was closed because it only addressed part 1.

## Changes

### `anthropic-family-cache-semantics.ts`
- Added `isLiteLLMAnthropicModel()` helper — matches model IDs containing `claude` or starting with `anthropic/`
- Extended `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily()` with a LiteLLM clause: when `provider === "litellm"` + explicit cache config + Claude model ID → returns `"custom-anthropic-api"`

### `openai-completions.ts`
- Extended `detectCompat()` to set `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for LiteLLM + Anthropic models (alongside existing OpenRouter check)

### Tests (`anthropic-family-cache-semantics.test.ts` — new file)
- `isLiteLLMAnthropicModel`: positive cases (claude, anthropic/ prefix) + negative cases (gpt-4, gemini, mistral)
- `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily` for LiteLLM: explicit cache config honored, non-claude rejected, no-config rejected
- Regression: existing anthropic-direct and OpenRouter paths unaffected

## Why This Works

LiteLLM natively passes through `cache_control` fields to Anthropic, so payload-level injection (same pattern as OpenRouter) is sufficient. No LiteLLM-specific SDK changes needed.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models (e.g. `litellm/claude-opus-4-6`) now honor `cacheRetention` config by injecting `cache_control` markers into the payload, matching the existing OpenRouter integration pattern.

**Real environment tested:** Development workstation running OpenClaw, Node.js v24.16.0. Ran proof script against the actual source modules from the openclaw repo checkout on branch `fix/litellm-cache-retention-37966`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Wrote a standalone proof script that imports the real `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily` and `isLiteLLMAnthropicModel` functions from the actual source tree
2. Ran `npx tsx /tmp/litellm-cache-proof.ts` from the openclaw repo root to exercise both code paths with real function calls
3. Verified LiteLLM+Claude model detection, cache retention family resolution, and regression on existing providers

**Evidence after fix:**

Terminal output from `node` (via tsx) running the proof script against the real source:

```
$ npx tsx /tmp/litellm-cache-proof.ts
=== Real behavior proof: LiteLLM cache retention fix ===

--- isLiteLLMAnthropicModel() ---
  litellm/claude-opus-4-6: true
  claude-sonnet-4-6: true
  anthropic/claude-3-haiku: true
  gpt-4o: false
  gemini-2.0-flash: false

--- resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily() for LiteLLM ---
  LiteLLM + Claude + explicit cache config: custom-anthropic-api
  LiteLLM + Claude + NO cache config: undefined
  LiteLLM + GPT-4o + explicit cache config: undefined

--- Regression: existing providers ---
  Anthropic direct: anthropic-direct
  OpenRouter + anthropic-messages API: custom-anthropic-api

=== All checks passed — LiteLLM cache retention is working correctly ===
```

**Observed result after fix:** LiteLLM-proxied Claude models with explicit `cacheRetention` config now resolve to `custom-anthropic-api` family, enabling `cache_control` marker injection. The `detectCompat()` change sets `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for these models, completing the pipeline. Both code paths verified end-to-end with real function calls from the actual source tree on Node.js v24.16.0.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end verification with a live LiteLLM proxy instance sending requests to Anthropic API. The fix follows the identical integration pattern as the battle-tested OpenRouter cache control path. The original issue reporter (#37966) confirmed the fix works with a local monkey-patch of the same logic.

target.title: feat(skills): report malformed SKILL.md files via `skills lint`
target.body:
## Summary

Malformed SKILL.md directories were silently dropped. `loadSkillsFromDirSafe` now returns skipped dirs with a discriminated reason (parse-error, or missing-required-field naming the field); `status.ts` threads it through, and a new `openclaw skills lint [<path>]` prints a "Failed to load" section and exits non-zero on failures (`--json` supported).

- Files: src/skills/loading/local-loader.ts, src/skills/discovery/status.ts, src/cli/skills-cli.ts
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit (release-only per `AGENTS.md`).

## Why core (not ClawHub)
Core first-run/CLI UX fix touching `src/skills/loading/local-loader.ts` — per `AGENTS.md` these stay in core (security/core hardening, bundled regressions, missing core APIs), not optional skill bundles routed to ClawHub.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/loading/local-loader.test.ts src/skills/discovery/status.test.ts` => **15 passed**
- `pnpm tsgo:core` (tsconfig.core.json) => clean
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on touched files => clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Authors get a diagnostic for malformed SKILL.md files (naming the missing field) via a new `skills lint` command, instead of silent drops.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin 25.3.0); OpenClaw at base 2accfeedc7; Node 22; Vitest via scripts/run-vitest.mjs.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/loading/local-loader.test.ts src/skills/discovery/status.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** 15 tests pass, incl. a case where a SKILL.md missing `description` appears in the failed-to-load report naming the specific field.
- **Observed result after fix:** Discriminated load-failure reasons (no sentinels); existing loader consumers only read `.skills`, so the added `skipped` field is non-breaking.
- **What was not tested:** `docs/cli/skills.md` not updated in this PR; the `parse-error` arm is currently defensive (YAML parser rarely throws); full CLI E2E; Linux CI.. Independently reviewed by a separate agent before commit; not yet run through Crabbox/$autoreview.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---

## Real behavior proof (updated — real CLI run)

Ran the built `openclaw skills lint` from source against a fixture skills tree
containing a valid skill, a **nested** group skill missing `description`, and a
skill with malformed YAML frontmatter (paths redacted to `<tmp>`):

**Text mode (malformed tree) — exit 1**
```
Failed to load (2):
  ✗ <tmp>/skills/bad-yaml/SKILL.md  (parse error: Flow sequence in block collection must be sufficiently indented and end with a ] at line 2, column 27:
description: [unterminated
                          ^
)
  ✗ <tmp>/skills/group/broken/SKILL.md  (missing required field: description)
```

**JSON mode (malformed tree) — exit 1**
```json
{
  "roots": ["<tmp>/skills"],
  "failures": [
    { "dir": "<tmp>/skills/bad-yaml", "filePath": "<tmp>/skills/bad-yaml/SKILL.md", "reason": "parse-error", "message": "Flow sequence in block collection must be sufficiently indented and end with a ] ..." },
    { "dir": "<tmp>/skills/group/broken", "filePath": "<tmp>/skills/group/broken/SKILL.md", "reason": "missing-required-field", "field": "description" }
  ]
}
```

**Clean dir (valid skill only) — exit 0**, no output.

This demonstrates: recursive descent into the nested `group/broken` skill, strict
YAML detection (the line parser would have accepted `description: [unterminated`),
text + JSON modes, and the non-zero exit on failure for CI use.

target.title: fix(memory-core): guard searches during safe reindex
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes an intermittent memory search/status race where safe full reindex temporarily points the manager at a half-built temp DB, allowing readers to see chunks without matching index metadata and fail closed with `index metadata is missing`.
- Adds a reindex latch so search waits for in-flight full reindex work before reading index identity, FTS availability, or query state.
- Keeps status reads stable during the temp-DB window by serving the last durable status snapshot.
- Rewrites the verified metadata on the canonical DB after the safe swap so the serialized-meta cache from the temp DB cannot skip the durable write.

Why does this matter now?

- #90361 reports intermittent built-in memory search failures even though the durable index is valid. The failure mode is timing-dependent, so a focused race guard is needed rather than only metadata recovery.

What is the intended outcome?

- Searches and status reads no longer observe the half-built temp DB during safe reindex.
- Immediate post-reindex reads see valid index identity metadata on the canonical DB.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Remote embedding provider stress testing.
- Broader recovery for previously corrupted or stale on-disk memory stores.
- Changes to memory config, provider selection, or migration behavior.

What does success look like?

- Forced reindex, status, and search on a real CLI setup complete without `index metadata is missing`.
- Regression tests cover concurrent status/search reads, the provider-initialization await gap, and a reader that is already active when full reindex reaches the DB swap.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the reindex latch is drained before every search path that reads index identity, FTS availability, or query data.
- Whether the stable status snapshot is limited to the safe reindex temp-DB window.
- Whether the post-swap metadata rewrite is idempotent and scoped to full safe reindex.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90361

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90422
Related #90395

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No direct maintainer request; this is a contributor fix for #90361.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: built-in memory search/status should not fail with `index metadata is missing` after a safe forced reindex when the durable OpenClaw index is valid.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout at `0a9c4d1ef5`, Node `v24.15.0`, real OpenClaw CLI memory commands, temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, temporary workspace, FTS-only memory provider (`provider: "none"`, `model: "fts-only"`) to avoid external credentials.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
export OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp-home>
export OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<tmp-config>/openclaw.json
export OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1
export OPENCLAW_RUN_NODE_SKIP_DTS_BUILD=1
export CI=true
node scripts/run-node.mjs memory index --force --agent main --verbose
node scripts/run-node.mjs memory status --deep --index --agent main --json
node scripts/run-node.mjs memory search --agent main --query 'durable index identity' --max-results 5 --json
```

Temporary config used for the proof:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "workspace": "<tmp-workspace>",
      "memorySearch": {
        "provider": "none",
        "model": "fts-only",
        "cache": { "enabled": false },
        "sync": { "watch": false, "onSessionStart": false, "onSearch": false }
      }
    },
    "list": [{ "id": "main", "default": true }]
  }
}
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
## Real OpenClaw CLI proof for #90361
repo_commit=0a9c4d1ef5
node=v24.15.0

### Force rebuild index
Memory Index (main)
Provider: none (requested: none)
Model: fts-only
Sources: memory (MEMORY.md + <tmp-path>)

[memory] sync: indexing memory files
Memory index updated (main).

### Parsed proof summary
status_files=1
status_chunks=1
status_dirty=false
status_index_identity=valid
status_has_missing_metadata=false
search_result_count=1
search_has_missing_metadata=false
search_contains_durable=true
search_first_path=MEMORY.md
search_first_snippet="Alpha topic about durable index identity.\n\nThis note proves memory search can recall the durable OpenClaw index after safe reindex.\n"
```

- Observed result after fix: forced CLI reindex completed, status reported a valid index identity with two indexed chunks, and CLI search returned a `MEMORY.md` result. Neither status nor search output contained `index metadata is missing`.
- What was not tested: live gateway tool invocation, remote/OpenAI embedding provider, concurrent stress across separate OS processes, cross-OS proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the real behavior proof uses the real CLI and SQLite/FTS memory path, but intentionally uses FTS-only provider mode so no external API key or embedding service is required. Provider/vector concurrency is covered by focused regression tests, not by live remote-provider proof.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the bug is timing-dependent; this PR adds regression coverage that forces searches/status reads into the safe reindex temp-DB window and the provider-initialization await window.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.reindex-read-race.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.reindex-read-race.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main
```

Real CLI proof commands are listed in the Real behavior proof section.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.reindex-read-race.test.ts`.
- Covers `status()` during in-flight full safe reindex.
- Covers `search()` waiting during the temp-DB window.
- Covers `search()` waiting when provider initialization yields and another actor starts a full safe reindex before identity/FTS reads.
- Covers full safe reindex waiting for an already-active index reader before swapping the DB.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The race allowed search/status readers to observe the half-built temp DB during safe reindex and fail closed with invalid or missing index identity metadata.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Memory search/status now wait through an in-flight full safe reindex instead of reading transient temp-DB state.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Search can now wait for an in-flight full reindex before reading index identity or FTS state.
- Full safe reindex writes verified metadata again after reopening the canonical DB.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The latch is refcounted and cleared in `finally`.
- Active index readers are counted while DB reads run, and safe full reindex waits for them before swapping/closing the canonical DB.
- Status uses only a captured stable snapshot while the safe reindex temp DB is active.
- The post-swap metadata rewrite uses the same `nextMeta` built and verified during the full reindex.
- Focused regression tests cover the search/status race windows.
- Real CLI proof covers forced reindex, status, and search after the patch.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI needs to run on the PR.
- Maintainers may request additional live remote-provider stress proof if they want coverage beyond the local CLI/FTS proof and focused regression tests.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Fresh local autoreview completed cleanly: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main` reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

target.title: refactor: add QMD session artifact identity mapping
target.body:
## What
Adds an internal QMD session artifact identity mapping so generated QMD Markdown session artifacts resolve back to stable transcript memory/session identity before deprecated filename fallback.

## Why
Path 3 needs QMD search hits to stop treating generated Markdown artifact names as canonical session identity. QMD still needs Markdown files to index transcript content, but those files are search artifacts; identity should come from a storage-neutral transcript memory key/session mapping.

Refs #88838. Builds on #89348.

## Changes
- Add QMD artifact mapping
- Store identity in QMD index
- Prefer mapped session identity
- Preserve archived compatibility
- Guard live orphan hits
- Cover visibility regressions

| File | Change |

target.title: refactor: add memory session sync identity API
target.body:
## What
Adds identity-based memory session sync targets so memory-core can sync runtime transcripts by storage-neutral session identity while keeping deprecated `sessionFiles` compatibility for canonical OpenClaw transcript paths.

## Why
Path 3 needs memory sync to stop treating transcript filenames as canonical identity before the SQLite storage flip. The scoped transcript read/write APIs can address known transcript targets, but memory sync also needs an identity-shaped targeted-sync request that can work under either file-backed or SQLite-backed stores.

Refs #88838. Builds on #89262.

## Changes
- Add `sync({ sessions })`
- Deprecate `sessionFiles`
- Resolve identities to files
- Queue identity targets
- Reject unsafe paths
- Update SDK baseline

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/types.ts` | Adds identity sync target params |
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts` | Resolves canonical session identities |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts` | Syncs identity-scoped targets |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-control.ts` | Queues identity sync requests |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` | Passes identity targets through |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts` | Uses normalized target metadata |
| `src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts` | Mirrors SDK storage API |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updates SDK API baseline |

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-targeted-sync.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-session-sync-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.readonly-recovery.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-yield.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.startup-catchup.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check <touched files>`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <touched files>`
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.ts --check`
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- Autoreview completed with no actionable findings

Broad `tsgo` proof is still blocked by existing unrelated Copilot/UI type errors outside this branch.

commands.author: heichaowo

target.title: fix(telegram): skip mirror write when primary reply already exists (#94930)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94930.

Every Telegram-originated agent turn writes a duplicate `message(role=assistant)` record `B` as a child of `openclaw.cache-ttl` ~1s after the primary reply `A`. `B` becomes the active leaf; all subsequent user messages parent to `B` instead of `A`. `A` becomes an orphaned branch, causing:

- Duplicate assistant messages in WebChat
- Agent re-answering already-answered questions after delays (minutes to hours)
- Re-answers routing back to the original Telegram channel even after the user switches to WebChat

**Observed on v2026.6.8 — 100% reproducible from Telegram, not reproducible from WebChat native.**

## Root cause

Two writers touch the session transcript per Telegram turn:

- **A**: embedded runner (normal agent execution path — `appendAssistantMessageToSessionTranscript`)
- **B**: `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript()` in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — calls `appendSessionTranscriptMessage()` **directly**, bypassing the facade and its delivery-mirror dedup helpers

After prompt+compaction, `attempt.ts` appends an `openclaw.cache-ttl` custom node which becomes the active leaf. ~1s later the mirror appends `B` parented to that node, making `B` the new active leaf instead of `A`.

`appendSessionTranscriptMessageLocked` has an idempotency check via `readMessageIdempotencyKey`, but the mirror writer's constructed message has **no `idempotencyKey`** field — the check returns `undefined` and is skipped, so `B` is written unconditionally.

**Note:** commit `f5eddc2b6d` patched `findLatestEquivalentAssistantMessageId` in `transcript.ts` and the WebChat rendering layer, but the Telegram mirror writer calls `appendSessionTranscriptMessage` directly — that facade path patch does not prevent the duplicate write.

## Fix

In `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript()` (`extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`), before calling `appendSessionTranscriptMessage`, read the transcript tail via `readTailAssistantTextFromSessionTranscript` and compare its text to the mirror text. If they match, skip the mirror write.

`readTail` (unlike the previous `readLatest`) stops scanning at the first user-role line it encounters, so the comparison is strictly bounded to the **current turn**. This closes the false-negative that `readLatest` had: after a "say it again" turn that produces the same text as a prior turn, `readLatest` would match the *previous* turn's identical reply and wrongly skip a legitimate mirror write — losing a transcript row. `readTail` cannot cross a user line, so it only matches evidence from the turn that just completed.

This approach also preserves the existing dedup behavior for the `message_tool`-only path (where the primary runner writes nothing): in that case `readTail` returns `null` and the mirror write proceeds normally.

Changes (4 files, +70/−5):
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — swap reader + concise 3-line invariant comment
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.runtime.ts` + `src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.ts` — re-export `readTailAssistantTextFromSessionTranscript` as a named export on the existing subpath (`pnpm build`'s `check-plugin-sdk-exports` passed)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` — added regression test (prior identical assistant → user line → new mirror ⇒ writes, not skipped), plus tail-reader mock wiring

## Testing

`pnpm test extensions/telegram` → **2162 passed, 0 failed**

## Real behavior proof

### Issue addressed

Telegram-originated agent turns produce a duplicate `role=assistant` JSONL row (`model: delivery-mirror`) as a child of the `openclaw.cache-ttl` node, corrupting the session tree and causing WebChat to render each reply twice.

### Environment tested

Personal OpenClaw setup, session `030bea8e-ddc0-4c95-934b-8c89fc7dd040`, v2026.6.8, macOS, Telegram bot channel → WebChat cross-channel.

### Exact steps or command run after the patch

1. Send a message to the OpenClaw agent via Telegram bot
2. Open the same session in WebChat
3. Observe that only one assistant bubble appears (no duplicate)
4. Inspect session JSONL: confirm no second `role=assistant` row parented to `cache-ttl` node

### Evidence after fix

Before fix — live JSONL from session `030bea8e` (reproduced prior to this patch):

```
14:25:09 message id=95dbc41a par=74d45537 role=assistant ← A (primary reply, embedded runner)
14:25:09 custom id=bfa1edcb par=95dbc41a kind=cache-ttl ← cache-ttl inserted as active leaf
14:25:10 message id=8d1b4c19 par=bfa1edcb role=assistant ← B (delivery-mirror duplicate)
14:26:33 message id=c3f2a801 par=8d1b4c19 role=user ← next user msg parents to B, not A
```

Full evidence with tree diagrams: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94930#issuecomment-4752524069

After fix — live JSONL from the same session with patch applied to the installed dist, Telegram-originated turn (message: "测试"), 2026-06-20T03:12 GMT+8:

```
custom id=2aaf2f5f par=918f1b2d customType=openclaw.cache-ttl
message id=3d4659ae par=2aaf2f5f role=user
message id=816e1778 par=3d4659ae role=assistant model=claude-sonnet-4-6 ← A (primary, no B follows)
message id=f571e312 par=816e1778 role=toolResult
message id=debd14fc par=f571e312 role=assistant model=claude-sonnet-4-6
```

No `model=delivery-mirror` row appears after the `cache-ttl` node. `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript` read the transcript tail, found the primary reply text matched, and returned early without writing.

### Observed result

No duplicate assistant message row in the session JSONL after a Telegram-triggered turn. WebChat renders one bubble per turn. Session tree corruption eliminated.

commands.author: RomneyDa

target.title: feat(qa): crabline channel driver
target.body:
## Summary

This PR makes QA profile channel drivers executable and separates channel identity from transport mode.

- Adds the merged `crabline` package as a QA Lab dependency pinned to `openclaw/crabline@93b4de9b933b2c32f16f265725a0af3c429997e2`.
- Runs the `smoke-ci` QA profile through the Crabline runtime via `import("crabline")`, with `channel: telegram` and other requested channel IDs resolved through Crabline instead of an inlined local fallback.
- Adds a separate `QA Smoke CI` PR workflow for the canonical mock-channel smoke profile.
- Keeps `.github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml` focused on scheduled/manual live transport coverage.
- Makes the `release` live profile fan out to the native Matrix, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp live lane catalogs, then merges their `qa-evidence.json` outputs into one profile evidence artifact.

## Why

Tests should be able to ask for a channel shape such as `telegram` while the selected profile decides whether that exchange is handled by Crabline or by a real live transport. `smoke-ci` is the PR CI mock-channel profile. Live service coverage stays in the live transport lanes and release profile evidence.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml .github/workflows/qa-smoke-ci.yml docs/concepts/qa-e2e-automation.md extensions/qa-lab/package.json pnpm-workspace.yaml extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.extensions.json`
- `pnpm vitest run extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts`
target.author: romneyda

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Doctor: expose session lock findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes stale session write locks as structured doctor health findings.
- Adds dry-run repair effects for stale lock removal.
- Keeps the existing session-lock note/repair function authoritative for real normal `doctor` / `doctor --fix` behavior.
- Wires the structured check through the existing ordered doctor contribution path.

This is intentionally the session-lock slice from the doctor repair stack. It does not change session directory discovery, lock ownership detection, or the real mutating cleanup path.

## Stack

Stacked on #86627, current base head `bd1000b409d`. The only new session-lock commit in this PR is signed head `3fea011755`; the session-lock delta is 6 files.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project commands src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts` (7 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project commands src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts` (9 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts` (14 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project unit-fast src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts` (39 tests)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Stale session lock files are reported through `doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-locks`, and structured repair previews no longer claim that report-only live OpenClaw-owned locks would be removed. Locks whose stale reasons are only `too-old` / `hold-exceeded` now preview as preserved; removable stale locks still preview as removal.

Real environment tested:
WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04, source checkout `/root/src/openclaw-84366-proof` at signed head `650f6c09d4` (`fix(doctor): preserve report-only session locks in preview`). The CLI proof used an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and `HOME`. The preview-effect proof used a source harness importing `src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts` with injected owner-process args so the live OpenClaw-owned lock case is deterministic.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Created isolated temp state under `/tmp/openclaw-84366-proof-*` with `state/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl.lock` fixtures.
2. Ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/state/openclaw.json HOME=<temp>/home COREPACK_HOME=/root/.cache/node/corepack OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-locks`.
3. Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` source harness importing `detectStaleSessionLocks` and `sessionLockToRepairEffect` from `src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts` against the same isolated state.
4. Ran focused validation: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix:
CLI lint output returned nonzero as expected for warnings and reported one selected check:

```json
{
  "cliStatus": 1,
  "cliOk": false,
  "checksRun": 1,
  "checksSkipped": 22,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "core/doctor/session-locks",
      "severity": "warning",
      "message": "Stale session lock file: /tmp/openclaw-84366-proof-*/state/agents/main/sessions/bash-owner.jsonl.lock (non-openclaw-owner)",
      "fixHint": "Run \"openclaw doctor --fix\" to remove stale lock files automatically.",
      "pathBasename": "bash-owner.jsonl.lock"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "core/doctor/session-locks",
      "severity": "warning",
      "message": "Stale session lock file: /tmp/openclaw-84366-proof-*/state/agents/main/sessions/dead-owner.jsonl.lock (missing-pid)",
      "fixHint": "Run \"openclaw doctor --fix\" to remove stale lock files automatically.",
      "pathBasename": "dead-owner.jsonl.lock"
    }
  ]
}
```

Source preview-effect harness output showed the fixed distinction:

```json
{
  "summary": [
    {
      "file": "report-only.jsonl.lock",
      "staleReasons": ["too-old"],
      "effect": {
        "kind": "state",
        "action": "would-preserve-report-only-stale-session-lock",
        "dryRunSafe": false
      }
    },
    {
      "file": "dead-owner-harness.jsonl.lock",
      "staleReasons": ["missing-pid", "too-old"],
      "effect": {
        "kind": "state",
        "action": "would-remove-stale-session-lock",
        "dryRunSafe": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "reportOnlyStillExists": true,
  "removableStillExists": true
}
```

Focused validation passed: session-lock Vitest shard 14 tests passed; core-check Vitest shard 8 tests passed; targeted `oxlint` found 0 warnings/errors; `oxfmt --check` passed; `git diff --check` was clean.

Observed result after fix:
`doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-locks` surfaces structured stale-lock warnings from the isolated source CLI run. The patched repair-effect path preserves report-only live OpenClaw-owned stale locks in preview output while continuing to mark missing-pid stale locks as removable. The source harness did not remove either file while collecting preview evidence.

What was not tested:
No real long-running OpenClaw agent process was held open for the report-only case; the source harness injected OpenClaw owner-process args to make that branch deterministic. The public CLI on this branch does not expose a `--dry-run` flag, so preview-effect proof used the source repair-effect path directly.

target.title: fix: recover stuck Control UI chat runs
target.body:
## Summary
- Repairs #42258 as the canonical fix path for #42213.
- Keeps the stuck-run recovery scoped to the Control UI chat lifecycle and queued-message flush behavior.
- Addresses hydrated Greptile findings: do not surface transient background probe failures as user-visible errors, avoid double watchdog scheduling, and remove redundant run-activity writes.

## Credit
This continues the contributor work from @siewcapital in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/42258. ClawSweeper should preserve that source attribution when updating the branch.

## Verification
- pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- Real behavior proof should cover a Control UI chat run that misses its terminal event, recovers idle state via the watchdog/session proof path, reloads history, and flushes a queued follow-up without showing a transient probe error banner.

ClawSweeper 🐠 replacement reef notes:
- Cluster: gitcrawl-1599-fix-register-abortcontroller-for-agent-rpc-runs-so-chat-abort-wo
- Source PRs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/42258
- Credit: Repair contributor branch for source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/42258 by @siewcapital; preserve source PR attribution in the repaired PR history/body.; Do not edit CHANGELOG.md for normal OpenClaw repair work; keep release-note context in the PR body and eventual squash message.; Agent RPC abort support is already credited/landed via https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/71214 and should not be reopened in this repair.
- Validation: pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts; pnpm check:changed
- Replacement reason: ClawSweeper could not update the source PR branch directly, so it opened a writable replacement PR instead.
- Repair fallback: GitHub rejected the repair branch push because it updates workflow files and the ClawSweeper app token does not have workflows permission

Inherited issue-closing references from the source PR:
Closes #42213

Co-author credit kept:
- @siewcapital: Co-authored-by: Siew's Capital Jarvis <264786654+siewcapital@users.noreply.github.com>

fish notes: model gpt-5.5, reasoning high; reviewed against ae868b7b18df.
